heading: Assaulting, resisting, or impeding certain officers or employees
label: 111 Assaulting, resisting, or impeding certain officers or employees
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 118, 254 (, ; , ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 6235 Stat. 1100May 18, 1934, ch. 299, § 248 Stat. 781This section consolidates sections 118 and 254 with changes in phraseology and substance necessary to effect the consolidation.Also the words “Bureau of Animal Industry of the Department of Agriculture” appearing in , U.S.C., 1940 ed., were inserted in enumeration of Federal officers and employees in .section 118 of title 18section 1114 of this titleThe punishment provision of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., was adopted as the latest expression of Congressional intent. This consolidation eliminates a serious incongruity in punishment and application.section 254 of title 18

Amendments2021—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 117–592008—Subsec. (a). substituted “where such acts involve physical contact with the victim of that assault or the intent to commit another felony” for “in all other cases” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 110–1772002—Subsec. (a). , substituted “8” for “three” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 107–273, § 11008(b)(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “20” for “ten”.Pub. L. 107–273, § 11008(b)(2)1996—Subsec. (b). inserted “(including a weapon intended to cause death or danger but that fails to do so by reason of a defective component)” after “deadly or dangerous weapon”.Pub. L. 104–1321994—Subsec. (a). , inserted “, where the acts in violation of this section constitute only simple assault, be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than one year, or both, and in all other cases,” after “shall” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 103–322, § 320101(a)(1)Subsec. (b). , inserted “or inflicts bodily injury” after “weapon”.Pub. L. 103–322, § 320101(a)(2)1988— amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 100–690“Whoever forcibly assaults, resists, opposes, impedes, intimidates, or interferes with any person designated in while engaged in or on account of the performance of his official duties, shall be fined not more than $5,000 or imprisoned not more than three years, or both.section 1114 of this title“Whoever, in the commission of any such acts uses a deadly or dangerous weapon, shall be fined not more than $10,000 or imprisoned not more than ten years, or both.”

C, title I, § 11008(a)116 Stat. 1818Nov. 2, 2002“This section [amending this section, sections 115 and 876 of this title, and provisions set out as a note under , Judiciary and Judicial Procedure] may be cited as the ‘Federal Judiciary Protection Act of 2002’.”section 994 of Title 28

children.text: forcibly assaults, resists, opposes, impedes, intimidates, or interferes with any person designated in while engaged in or on account of the performance of official duties; orsection 1114 of this title
forcibly assaults or intimidates any person who formerly served as a person designated in section 1114 on account of the performance of official duties during such person’s term of service,

children.heading: .—Enhanced Penalty
children.label: b .—Enhanced Penalty
children.text: Whoever, in the commission of any acts described in subsection (a), uses a deadly or dangerous weapon (including a weapon intended to cause death or danger but that fails to do so by reason of a defective component) or inflicts bodily injury, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 20 years, or both.

children.heading: .—Extraterritorial Jurisdiction
children.label: c .—Extraterritorial Jurisdiction
children.text: There is extraterritorial jurisdiction over the conduct prohibited by this section.
